perturbation.original_text: 2.9. No later than the Site Commitment Dates set forth in Exhibit "B", Developer shall submit for the Approved Site to Franchisor for its review and approval of: a. A fully negotiated but unexecuted lease, which may only subject to obtaining necessary governmental permits. The unexecuted form of the lease must be submitted to Franchisor to review for the required terms and conditions listed in Sections 2.9, 2.10, 2.11 and 2.12 below prior to full execution of the lease. Franchisor will promptly notify Developer upon their approval of the inclusion of such required terms and conditions. Developer will promptly then provide a final executed copy of the lease to Franchisor; or b. A purchase agreement. Should Developer purchase the site using another entity other than the franchise entity, Developer must then enter into a lease with the Franchise entity as the lessee and the purchasing entity as the lessor and must comply with all the requirements of this Sections 2.9, 2.10, 2.11 and 2.12 below).
perturbation.changed_text: 2.9. No later than the Site Commitment Dates set forth in Exhibit "B", Developer shall submit for the Approved Site to Franchisor for its review and approval of: a. A fully negotiated but unexecuted lease, which may only subject to obtaining necessary governmental permits. The unexecuted form of the lease must be submitted to Franchisor to review for the required terms and conditions listed in Sections 2.10, 2.11 and 2.12 below prior to full execution of the lease. Franchisor will promptly notify Developer upon their approval of the inclusion of such required terms and conditions. Developer will promptly then provide a final executed copy of the lease to Franchisor; or b. A purchase agreement. Should Developer purchase the site using another entity other than the franchise entity, Developer must then enter into a lease with the Franchise entity as the lessee and the purchasing entity as the lessor and must comply with all the requirements of this Sections 2.10, 2.11 and 2.12 below).
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the reference to 'Sections 2.9' in the cross-reference for lease requirements, the modified text creates an in-text contradiction. Section 2.9 outlines crucial steps for submitting the lease to Franchisor for approval, and by excluding it from the listed sections, the requirement to follow the terms outlined within 2.9 creates ambiguity. The Franchisee can claim that since it wasn't specifically stated to follow the requirements in section 2.9 they can choose to ignore its outline, potentially creating a contradiction with the overall intention of this clause which is to ensure the lease is following the requirements from El Pollo Loco's perspective.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.9. No later than the Site Commitment Dates set forth in Exhibit "B", Developer shall submit for the Approved Site to Franchisor for its review and approval of: a. A fully negotiated but unexecuted lease, which may only subject to obtaining necessary governmental permits. The unexecuted form of the lease must be submitted to Franchisor to review for the required terms and conditions listed in Sections 2.10, 2.11 and 2.12 below prior to full execution of the lease. Franchisor will promptly notify Developer upon their approval of the inclusion of such required terms and conditions. Developer will promptly then provide a final executed copy of the lease to Franchisor; or b. A purchase agreement. Should Developer purchase the site using another entity other than the franchise entity, Developer must then enter into a lease with the Franchise entity as the lessee and the purchasing entity as the lessor and must comply with all the requirements of this Sections 2.10, 2.11 and 2.12 below).
perturbation.justification: The changed text omits the reference to 'Sections 2.9' in the cross-reference for lease requirements, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which is identical to the original text. The contradiction lies in that the developer is no longer explicitly instructed to follow the guidelines in Section 2.9, which outlines the procedures for submitting the lease to Franchisor for approval. The changed text therefore introduces a conflict that would cause confusion and be a strong contradiction, rendering the obligations unclear.

perturbation.original_text: 20.1. To further protect the El Pollo Loco® System while this Agreement is in effect, Developer and each officer, director, shareholder, member, manager, partner and other equity owner, as applicable, of Developer, if Developer is an entity, shall neither directly nor indirectly own, operate, control or any financial interest in any other business which would constitute a "Competitive Business" (as hereinafter defined) without the prior written consent of Franchisor; provided further, that Franchisor may, as its sole and absolute right, consent to the Developer's continued operation of any business already in existence and operating at the time of execution of this Agreement. In addition, Developer covenants that, except as otherwise approved in writing by the Franchisor, Developer shall not, for a continuous, uninterrupted period commencing upon the expiration, termination or assignment of this Agreement, regardless of the cause for termination, and continuing for two (2) years thereafter, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through or on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, partnership, corporation or other entity, own, operate, control or have any financial interest in any Competitive Business which is located or has outlets or restaurant units within the Territory. The foregoing shall not apply to operation of an El Pollo Loco® restaurant by Developer pursuant to a Franchise Agreement with Franchisor or the ownership by Developer of less than five percent (5%) of the issued or outstanding stock of any company whose shares are listed for trading on any public exchange or on the over-the-counter market, provided that Developer does not control or become involved in the operations of any such company. For purposes of this Section 20.1, a Competitive Business shall mean a self-service restaurant or fast-food business which sells chicken and/or Mexican food products, which products individually or collectively represent more than twenty percent (20%) of the revenues from such self-service restaurant or fast-food business operated at any one location during any calendar quarter. A "Competitive Business" shall not include a full-service restaurant.
perturbation.changed_text: 20.1. To further protect the El Pollo Loco® System while this Agreement is in effect, Developer and each officer, director, shareholder, member, manager, partner and other equity owner, as applicable, of Developer, if Developer is an entity, shall neither directly nor indirectly own, operate, control or any financial interest in any other business without the prior written consent of Franchisor; provided further, that Franchisor may, as its sole and absolute right, consent to the Developer's continued operation of any business already in existence and operating at the time of execution of this Agreement. In addition, Developer covenants that, except as otherwise approved in writing by the Franchisor, Developer shall not, for a continuous, uninterrupted period commencing upon the expiration, termination or assignment of this Agreement, regardless of the cause for termination, and continuing for two (2) years thereafter, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through or on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, partnership, corporation or other entity, own, operate, control or have any financial interest in any Competitive Business which is located or has outlets or restaurant units within the Territory. The foregoing shall not apply to operation of an El Pollo Loco® restaurant by Developer pursuant to a Franchise Agreement with Franchisor or the ownership by Developer of less than five percent (5%) of the issued or outstanding stock of any company whose shares are listed for trading on any public exchange or on the over-the-counter market, provided that Developer does not control or become involved in the operations of any such company. For purposes of this Section 20.1, a Competitive Business shall mean a self-service restaurant or fast-food business which sells chicken and/or Mexican food products, which products individually or collectively represent more than twenty percent (20%) of the revenues from such self-service restaurant or fast-food business operated at any one location during any calendar quarter. A "Competitive Business" shall not include a full-service restaurant.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase 'which would constitute a 'Competitive Business' (as hereinafter defined)' from the initial clause, the modified text creates an overbroad restriction. This introduces an in-text contradiction because the second half of the paragraph relies on this definition of 'Competitive Business' to specify the scope of the non-compete. Now, the first part of the section appears to prohibit owning *any* business, regardless of whether it competes with El Pollo Loco, while the later part of the section defines what a 'Competitive Business' is and seemingly limits the restriction to that definition. This creates uncertainty about what types of businesses are actually prohibited. The developer can make the argument that the contract is overly broad and restricts them from owning any business, or make the argument that the initial phrase doesn't need to be followed because the 'as hereinafter defined' part wasn't defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 20.1. To further protect the El Pollo Loco® System while this Agreement is in effect, Developer and each officer, director, shareholder, member, manager, partner and other equity owner, as applicable, of Developer, if Developer is an entity, shall neither directly nor indirectly own, operate, control or any financial interest in any other business without the prior written consent of Franchisor; provided further, that Franchisor may, as its sole and absolute right, consent to the Developer's continued operation of any business already in existence and operating at the time of execution of this Agreement. In addition, Developer covenants that, except as otherwise approved in writing by the Franchisor, Developer shall not, for a continuous, uninterrupted period commencing upon the expiration, termination or assignment of this Agreement, regardless of the cause for termination, and continuing for two (2) years thereafter, either directly or indirectly, for itself, or through or on behalf of, or in conjunction with any person, partnership, corporation or other entity, own, operate, control or have any financial interest in any Competitive Business which is located or has outlets or restaurant units within the Territory. The foregoing shall not apply to operation of an El Pollo Loco® restaurant by Developer pursuant to a Franchise Agreement with Franchisor or the ownership by Developer of less than five percent (5%) of the issued or outstanding stock of any company whose shares are listed for trading on any public exchange or on the over-the-counter market, provided that Developer does not control or become involved in the operations of any such company. For purposes of this Section 20.1, a Competitive Business shall mean a self-service restaurant or fast-food business which sells chicken and/or Mexican food products, which products individually or collectively represent more than twenty percent (20%) of the revenues from such self-service restaurant or fast-food business operated at any one location during any calendar quarter. A "Competitive Business" shall not include a full-service restaurant.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'which would constitute a "Competitive Business" (as hereinafter defined)' from the first sentence of Section 20.1 broadens the restriction on the developer's business activities. This conflicts with the remainder of Section 20.1, which defines and limits the scope of the restriction to 'Competitive Business'. The change makes it unclear whether the developer is prohibited from owning *any* business or only a 'Competitive Business', as defined later in the section, creating a direct inconsistency within the document. The contradicted text, specifically the second sentence of Section 20.1, still relies on the later definition of 'Competitive Business' which creates a contradiction with the changed text's initial broad restriction.
